mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly supporting higher equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0%, consumer spending growth decelerates sharply, reducing corporate revenue expectations and triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of observations where UNRATE crosses above 7.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts signal broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving corporate earnings growth and producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >55% of triggered months, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during late-cycle expansions, producing negative 20-day returns in >60% of months when INDPRO growth exceeds 2.0% year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 215.0, inflation expectations accelerate, prompting markets to price in imminent monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations and yields negative returns over five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL crosses the 215.0 threshold, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent increase in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer confidence and corporate earnings to drive positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative 60-day returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects sustained macroeconomic expansion, directly supporting higher corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters following positive GDPC1 growth, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 95.0, manufacturing sector contraction signals weakening economic momentum, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where INDPRO drops below 95.0, or the threshold signal fails to achieve statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.
